## Metadata
- File path: .github/workflows/diffdoc-summarize.yml
- File name: diffdoc-summarize.yml
- Extension: .yml
- Inferred language/type: YAML (GitHub Actions workflow)
- Content hash: 20868a449d460e028f0dd093cc3c0986
- Line count: 69
- Byte size: 2272
- Summary format: structured-functional-v1
- Notable symbols/classes/functions: None identified.
- External dependencies: actions/checkout@v4, actions/setup-node@v4
- Internal dependencies: None identified.
- Public API/exports: None identified.

## Purpose
This file defines a GitHub Actions workflow that automatically updates the DiffDoc manifest when changes are pushed to the main branch of the repository.

## User-Visible Behavior
When a push occurs to the main branch (excluding certain paths), the workflow checks out the repository, sets up Node.js, installs dependencies, builds the DiffDoc, updates the summary manifest, and commits any changes to the manifest back to the repository.

## Business Rules
The workflow ignores changes in specific paths related to DiffDoc artifacts. It only commits changes to the manifest if there are actual modifications detected in the relevant files.

## Data Inputs And Outputs
Inputs include environment variables for AI providers, DiffDoc base directory, and various model endpoints. Outputs include the updated DiffDoc manifest and summaries that are committed back to the repository.

## Side Effects
The workflow modifies the repository by committing changes to the DiffDoc manifest and summaries if there are updates detected.

## Error And Edge Cases
If there are no changes to the DiffDoc artifacts, the workflow exits without making any commits. There are no explicit error handling mechanisms defined for other potential failures.

## Dependencies
The workflow relies on external GitHub Actions for checking out the repository and setting up Node.js. It also depends on various environment variables and secrets for configuration.

## Operational Notes
The workflow runs on an Ubuntu environment and uses Node.js version 22. It is designed to avoid concurrent executions by grouping under "diffdoc-summarize-main". The caching of npm dependencies is enabled to improve performance.
